stemHtml: At high altitudes, the presence of water in the form of ice crystals can pose what potential hazards, if any, to an aircraft?

options.a: Ice crystals do not present a hazard to aircraft since they do NOT adhere to the airframe surfaces.
options.b: The ice crystals can accumulate on antenna systems and affect the transmission and reception of communication and navigation signals.
options.c: The crystals can accumulate on the wing's leading edge, changing the airflow over it and increasing the weight.
options.d: The crystals can accumulate on forward-facing parts of the engine and partially melt as they pass through the engine core, causing damage.
